description: BIS Standard for Talc used in Cosmetic Industry (Fourth Revision). Includes maximum limits for heavy metals and strict prohibition of Asbestos.

rules.name: Asbestos Ban
rules.description: Natural talcum deposits can contain asbestos, a known carcinogen. BIS IS 1462:2019 and FDA strictly ban asbestos in cosmetic talc. Zero tolerance.

rules.name: Mercury Ban
rules.description: Mercury and its compounds are strictly banned in all cosmetics, including powders, under BIS, EU, and CDSCO regulations.

rules.name: Lead Limit
rules.description: Lead (Pb) impurity must not exceed 20 ppm in cosmetic powders as per BIS Heavy Metals Schedule.

rules.name: Arsenic Limit
rules.description: Arsenic (As) trace heavy metal limit in talcum powder must not exceed 2 ppm (2 mg/kg).

rules.name: Heavy Metals Cumulative Limit
rules.description: Cumulative sum of Lead + Arsenic must not exceed 22 ppm to prevent dermal and systemic heavy metal burden.

rules.name: Moisture Content (Loss on Drying)
rules.description: Talc must have minimal moisture (Loss on Drying) to prevent microbial growth and powder clumping. Max 1.0%.

rules.name: Crystalline Silica Limit
rules.description: Respirable silica poses an inhalation hazard. Cosmetic talc should contain less than 1.0% crystalline silica.

rules.name: Parabens Limit
rules.description: Methylparaben / Propylparaben used as preservatives should not exceed 0.4% individually per EU Cosmetic Directive.

rules.name: Total Fragrance Limit
rules.description: Hypoallergenic and cosmetic powders should keep fragrance levels below 0.5% to avoid dermal irritation.

rules.name: Cosmetic Talc Content (Minimum)
rules.description: Cosmetic-grade hydrated magnesium silicate (talc) must constitute at least 95% of the powder formulation for purity compliance.

rules.name: Zinc Oxide Absorbent Limit
rules.description: Zinc oxide used as an absorbent should not exceed 5% in cosmetic talc formulations.

rules.name: Magnesium Carbonate Limit
rules.description: Magnesium carbonate as an absorbent filler should not exceed 5% in cosmetic talcum powder.
